topic: An archer demonstrating the Khatra technique upon releasing an arrow, slow-motion side view.

parsed_generation.script:
Scene 1 — Setup (0-2s): Side view of a lone archer, bow drawn, string taut; calm focus on grip and alignment.
Scene 2 — Release (2-5s): Slow-motion side view; archer executes the Khatra technique, fingers release, arrow shoots forward in a precise arc.
Scene 3 — Flight (5-8s): Side tracking shot follows the arrow in flight; archer remains still, posture reaffirmed, gaze locked on target.
parsed_generation.narration: The Khatra technique blends breath, tension, and release for precision. Slow-motion makes the subtle timing of fingers, string, and spine visible. Centered on a single archer, the sequence highlights how posture and timing shape the arrow's path.

feedback: The evaluation shows solid topic adherence and a clear archer-centric arc. To strengthen it, define the Khatra mechanics more explicitly with on-screen cues (finger placement, string tension, and breath). Consider broader camera coverage beyond the side view (e.g., close-ups of the fingers and face during release) and ensure the slow-motion emphasizes release timing without redundant narration.
fixes: Define Khatra mechanics with on-screen cues (finger placement, string tension, breath cycle)
